content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes input for an array of 5 integers and calculates the sum of all elements in the array. Print the sum.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains five space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of the five integers on a new line.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simply iterating over each test case, summing up the five integers in each case, and then printing the sum. This approach works because the constraints ensure that the sum of the five integers will always fit within the range of an integer data type.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 5) = O(T), because for each of the T test cases, we perform a constant amount of work (summing 5 integers).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1), because we only use a constant amount of space to store the sum and the current integer being processed, regardless of the input size.

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())):
    nums = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().strip().split()))
    print(sum(nums))
